normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU1_sorted_precond (a : Arr) : Sorted a → True := by intro _; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input slice is sorted in nondecreasing order (Sorted a).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_some_implies_index_valid_and_eq (a : Arr) (t : Int) :
  ∀ i : Nat,
    binarySearch a t = some i → IndexValid i a ∧ nthLe a i = t := by
  intro i h
  -- proof obligation: connect the model result to index validity and equality
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns Some i, then i is in bounds and arr[i] equals the target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_some_implies_eq_only (a : Arr) (t : Int) :
  ∀ i : Nat,
    binarySearch a t = some i → nthLe a i = t := by
  intro i h
  have : IndexValid i a ∧ nthLe a i = t := by
    -- from the stronger obligation, extract equality
    admit
  exact this.2
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns Some i, then arr[i] equals the target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_none_implies_absent (a : Arr) (t : Int) :
  binarySearch a t = none → ¬ (∃ i : Nat, IndexValid i a ∧ nthLe a i = t) := by
  intro h hn
  rcases hn with ⟨i, hi, hEq⟩
  -- proof obligation: show contradiction with the model behavior
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns None, then there is no in-bounds index i with arr[i] = target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU4_termination (a : Arr) (t : Int) : True := by trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The algorithm terminates on every input (lo..hi shrinks each iteration).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_duplicates_allow_any_valid_index (a : Arr) (t : Int) :
  ∀ i j : Nat,
    binarySearch a t = some i → binarySearch a t = some j →
      (IndexValid i a ∧ nthLe a i = t) ∧ (IndexValid j a ∧ nthLe a j = t) := by
  intro i j h1 h2
  -- proof obligation: both returned indices must be valid and equal to target
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If duplicates exist, returning any valid matching index is acceptable; both returned indices (when Some) must satisfy the success condition.
